gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A structured failure record carried by [`Verdict::Fail`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the `std.testing` representation of a diagnostic record (spec §3:"] # [doc = " `Fail { diagnostic: Diag }`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG-DIAG (RESOLVED, testing↔diag seam — spec §7-Q2)"] # [doc = " `std.diag` (M-510) has landed, so `FailRecord` now **delegates** to its canonical record:"] # [doc = " [`FailRecord::to_diag`] projects to a [`mycelium_diag::Diag`] — the structured diagnostic the"] # [doc = " rest of the failure-legibility substrate speaks (README §5). `FailRecord` keeps the"] # [doc = " **testing-specific reproduction metadata** (the seed + trial index) that a generic `Diag` does"] # [doc = " not model, and folds them — with the description and op context — into the `Diag`'s message and"] # [doc = " notes. The `Diag` is the legibility artifact; this record is its seed-reproducible test wrapper."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 — never-silent"] # [doc = " Every failure is a structured record with a description + reproducing seed — never an opaque"] # [doc = " red/green bit (RFC-0013 via spec §4.1 C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 — EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " The `description` carries the shrunk counterexample or diff; the `seed` makes the failure"] # [doc = " reproducible; the `context` names the op that failed. This is the EXPLAIN artifact."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct FailRecord { # [doc = " Human-readable description of the failure (shrunk counterexample, diff, etc.)."] # [doc = " **EXPLAIN artifact** (C3/G11/SC-3)."] pub description : String , # [doc = " The seed that reproduces this failure (RT3 / spec §3 — \"reproducible by seed\")."] pub seed : u64 , # [doc = " The trial index at which the failure occurred."] pub trial : u32 , # [doc = " The op context that produced this failure (e.g. `\"for_all\"`, `\"golden(name)\"`)."] pub context : String , }

gaps.snippet: impl FailRecord { # [doc = " Project this failure to the canonical [`mycelium_diag::Diag`] record (the testing↔diag"] # [doc = " seam — spec §7-Q2). The `description` becomes the diagnostic message; the op `context`, the"] # [doc = " reproducing `seed`, and the `trial` index ride along as EXPLAIN notes (G11). Severity is"] # [doc = " `Error` and the code is the test-failure class — never an opaque red/green bit (C1/C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (a pure, total projection)"] # [must_use] pub fn to_diag (& self) -> mycelium_diag :: Diag { mycelium_diag :: Diag :: error (mycelium_diag :: Code :: Other ("test.fail" . to_owned ())) . message (self . description . clone ()) . note (format ! ("context={}" , self . context)) . note (format ! ("seed={}" , self . seed)) . note (format ! ("trial={}" , self . trial)) } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `to_diag` signature: qualified type path `mycelium_diag :: Diag` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The reason a test was skipped (spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `Skipped` verdict always carries a reason — **never** an absent or unnamed skip (C1/G2)."] # [doc = " The reason is part of the EXPLAIN artifact; a skip without a reason would be a black box"] # [doc = " (C3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum SkipReason { # [doc = " The test is explicitly marked as ignored (e.g. `#[ignore]` equivalent)."] Ignored , # [doc = " A precondition for this test was not met (e.g. a required feature is absent)."] UnmetPrecondition , # [doc = " The golden baseline does not exist and must be recorded before the test can run."] # [doc = " **A missing baseline is `NeedsRecord`, never a silent auto-accept (C1/G2 — the golden"] # [doc = " test honesty crux).**"] NeedsRecord , # [doc = " A required backend for a differential test is not available in this environment."] # [doc = " **An unavailable backend is `BackendUnavailable`, never a silent pass (C1/G2 — the"] # [doc = " differential test honesty crux).**"] BackendUnavailable , # [doc = " A tool required to run this test is missing from the environment."] ToolMissing , }
gaps.reason: enum `SkipReason` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The reason a test result is undetermined (ran but could not decide — spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Undetermined` is distinct from both `Pass` and `Skipped`:"] # [doc = " - `Skipped` = could not run."] # [doc = " - `Undetermined` = ran but could not reach a verdict."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A non-decision is **never** a `Pass` (C1/G2)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum UndetReason { # [doc = " The oracle required for a differential check was unavailable at runtime."] OracleUnavailable , # [doc = " The property budget was exhausted without finding a counterexample, but the search was"] # [doc = " inconclusive (e.g. the generator space was undersampled relative to the property's domain)."] BudgetExhaustedInconclusive , # [doc = " The input to a differential or property is non-deterministic and could not be replayed."] # [doc = " **A flaky non-deterministic input is `NonDeterministicInput`, never a silent pass (RT3 /"] # [doc = " C6 — the seeded-generator reproducibility discipline).**"] NonDeterministicInput , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The outcome of a single test case (spec §3 / §4 guarantee matrix)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **The honesty crux:** `Skipped` and `Undetermined` are **first-class variants**, not flavours"] # [doc = " of `Pass`. The aggregator ([`crate::summarize`]) keeps their counts distinct; [`crate::is_green`]"] # [doc = " surfaces them. \"Green\" therefore means *checked and passed*, never *did not check* (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 — EXPLAIN"] # [doc = " Every non-`Pass` variant is a reified, inspectable artifact:"] # [doc = " - `Fail` — carries a [`FailRecord`] with description, seed, trial, context."] # [doc = " - `Skipped` — carries a [`SkipReason`] (never a nameless skip)."] # [doc = " - `Undetermined` — carries an [`UndetReason`] (never a nameless non-decision)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Verdict { # [doc = " The check ran and all assertions passed."] Pass , # [doc = " The check ran and at least one assertion failed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carries a [`FailRecord`] with the structured diagnostic: description, reproducing seed,"] # [doc = " trial index, and op context. Use [`FailRecord::to_diag`] to project to the canonical"] # [doc = " [`mycelium_diag::Diag`] record (FLAG-DIAG RESOLVED — `std.diag` has landed, M-510)."] Fail { # [doc = " The structured failure record (spec §3 `Fail { diagnostic: Diag }`)."] record : FailRecord , } , # [doc = " The check could not run. **Reported, never silently absent (C1/G2 — the honesty crux).**"] Skipped { # [doc = " Why the check was skipped (never anonymous — C3/G11)."] reason : SkipReason , } , # [doc = " The check ran but could not reach a verdict (e.g. oracle unavailable, budget exhausted"] # [doc = " inconclusively). **Not a `Pass` (C1/G2).**"] Undetermined { # [doc = " Why the verdict could not be determined."] reason : UndetReason , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `Verdict` variant `Fail` named field(s) `record` emitted positionally as `Fail(FailRecord)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: impl Summary { # [doc = " Total number of verdicts in this summary."] # [must_use] pub fn total (& self) -> u32 { self . passed . saturating_add (self . failed) . saturating_add (self . skipped) . saturating_add (self . undetermined) } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `total` body: Rust `.saturating_add` is saturating (silent clamp) — Mycelium has no saturating prim surface; desugaring to `saturating_add(…)` would fabricate an unknown prim or lie about overflow (G2/VR-5).
